sanskrit: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् । यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य अन्तकालमें भी मेरा स्मरण करते हुए शरीर छोड़कर जाता है, वह मेरे स्वरुप को ही प्राप्त होता है, इसमें सन्देह नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: Whoever remembers Me at the end and leaves the body attains My state; there is no doubt.
essence: The last remembered presence shapes the next state of being.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who remembers Krishna at the moment of death and leaves the body reaches Krishna's state without doubt.
simpleInsight: The final thought matters most, so the mind can be trained toward what is highest.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is hearing Krishna describe the inner condition that shapes death itself. After explaining the body, the self, and the sacred act, Krishna now gives the decisive promise: remembrance at the final moment carries a person beyond bodily ending.
modernRelevance: You are sitting beside a hospital bed, trying to stay steady while everything is slipping away. What the mind holds in that last, fragile moment may matter more than the words anyone says.
translationLiteral: अन्तकाले (at the end of life) / च (and) / माम् (Me) / एव (indeed) / स्मरन् (remembering) / मुक्त्वा (having छोड़कर / leaving) / कलेवरम् (the body) / यः (who) / प्रयाति (goes forth) / सः (that one) / मद्भावम् (My state) / याति (attains) / न (not) / अस्ति (is) / अत्र (here) / संशयः (doubt)
insight: It shifts attention from physical ending to the quality of final remembrance.

quoteVariants.text: Remember Me at death. You will come to My state.
The last thought carries you farther than the body.
